cases.action: Open the agent Dashboard (Bridge) and sign in as the admin
cases.see: You land on the dashboard with your name shown

cases.action: Try to sign in with the wrong password
cases.see: A clear error; you stay on the sign-in page

cases.action: In Settings → Branding/General, change the app name, colours, and the sign-in page layout, then save
cases.see: The portal and the sign-in page reflect your changes

cases.action: Upload a logo image
cases.see: The logo shows on the portal / sign-in page and is not broken

cases.action: In Settings → Branding, change primary & accent colours with the pickers and save
cases.see: The live portal mockup and the real portal update to the new colours

cases.action: Use “Extract from image”, upload a logo, and apply an extracted swatch as the primary colour
cases.see: Up to ~5 colour swatches appear; the chosen one becomes the primary colour

cases.action: Use “Extract from URL”, enter your website, and scan
cases.see: Brand colours are pulled from the site and offered as swatches

cases.action: Enter an invalid/unreachable URL in “Extract from URL” and scan
cases.see: A clear error; the app does not hang

cases.action: Set the portal tagline and email display name, then save
cases.see: The tagline shows on the portal; outbound email “From” uses the display name

cases.action: Set the portal sign-in page layout to “Minimal” and save
cases.see: The portal sign-in page shows a centred form with the logo

cases.action: Switch the sign-in layout to “Branded”, add a hero headline + 1–5 feature bullets, save, then open the portal sign-in page
cases.see: The sign-in page shows the split hero with your headline and bullets

cases.action: Choose “Branded” but leave the hero headline (or all features) empty
cases.see: Save is blocked/disabled until the required hero fields are filled

cases.action: Configure the two ticket-form dropdowns (label + options) and save, then open the portal submit form
cases.see: The configured dropdowns with your options appear on the submit form

cases.action: As a non-admin agent, open Branding settings
cases.see: Editing controls are hidden/read-only

cases.action: Settings → Email → connect your mailbox via Google or Microsoft
cases.see: Shows ‘Connected’ with the provider and email address

cases.action: Start (or let it auto-run) the archive/backfill of past mail
cases.see: A progress bar advances and completes

cases.action: Disconnect, then reconnect the mailbox
cases.see: State flips to disconnected, then back to connected

cases.action: Connect a Microsoft (Outlook/365) mailbox instead of Google
cases.see: Shows “Connected via Microsoft” with the address; mail begins to sync

cases.action: During the archive/backfill, click Cancel, then Resume
cases.see: Progress pauses, then continues from where it left off (not from zero)

cases.action: Start connecting, then cancel/deny at the provider consent screen
cases.see: You return to Settings → Email with a clear error you can dismiss; no mailbox is connected

cases.action: Click Disconnect and read the confirmation dialog before confirming
cases.see: Dialog explains sync stops but existing tickets stay; confirming returns to the connect screen

cases.action: As a non-admin agent, open Settings → Email
cases.see: No connect/disconnect controls; a message says to ask an admin

cases.action: Settings → GitHub → connect with a token
cases.see: Shows ‘Connected’; your repositories load in the dropdown

cases.action: After connecting, pick a default repository and save (confirming the change)
cases.see: The repo is saved; the search/dropdown lists your accessible repos

cases.action: Copy the webhook URL, generate a webhook secret, and reveal/regenerate it
cases.see: URL copies to clipboard; secret generates, can be revealed briefly, and regenerates on confirm

cases.action: Change the “fix-deployed” and “pending-confirmation” label names and save
cases.see: The label badges update and persist after reload

cases.action: Disconnect GitHub
cases.see: Status flips to “Not connected”

cases.action: Settings → AI Assistant → start a crawl/scan of your docs site URL
cases.see: Progress advances and pages/sources appear

cases.action: Confirm/approve embedding of the scanned pages
cases.see: Sources show as indexed / ready

cases.action: Add a single page by URL
cases.see: That page gets indexed

cases.action: Enter an invalid or unreachable URL
cases.see: A clear error; the app doesn’t hang or freeze

cases.action: Reindex a source, then delete one
cases.see: The source list updates accordingly

cases.action: After a scan completes, review the “ready to embed” summary (pages/sections/estimated cost)
cases.see: Counts and an estimated cost show, with Activate and Discard options

cases.action: Discard a completed scan instead of activating it
cases.see: The scanned pages are dropped and the panel returns to idle

cases.action: Start a scan and cancel it mid-way
cases.see: Scanning stops and the panel returns to idle

cases.action: On an indexed KB, click “Check for updates”
cases.see: Only changed/new pages are re-scanned; unchanged pages are skipped

cases.action: Use the status tabs (All/Ready/Scanned/Failed) and the search box in the sources list
cases.see: The sources table filters to matches

cases.action: Use “Remove all documents” and confirm
cases.see: All sources are cleared and the empty state shows

cases.action: Enable the first-responder bot and set its behaviour in AI Assistant
cases.see: Settings save and show the bot as enabled

cases.action: In Settings → Agents, set the AI first-responder fallback agent and save
cases.see: The fallback agent is saved (receives escalations when no shift is active)

cases.action: In AI Assistant, set/update the Gemini API key
cases.see: The key saves and shows as masked; “Saved” confirmation appears

cases.action: Disable the first-responder bot and save
cases.see: Settings show the bot as disabled (new tickets get no auto-reply)

cases.action: Settings → Shifts → add a shift with a primary agent and timezone
cases.see: The shift is listed

cases.action: Settings → Agents → invite an agent and set their role
cases.see: The agent appears in the list (pending/accepted)

cases.action: Deactivate, then reactivate an agent
cases.see: Their status toggles

cases.action: Sign in as a non-admin agent and look for admin Settings/actions
cases.see: Admin-only settings/actions are hidden or blocked

cases.action: Set the shifts timezone and save
cases.see: Shift times are interpreted in that timezone

cases.action: Create a shift whose start time is after its end time (e.g. 22:00–06:00)
cases.see: An “overnight shift — spans midnight” hint shows; the shift saves

cases.action: Toggle a shift inactive/active, then delete a shift
cases.see: The shift dims when inactive and disappears when deleted

cases.action: Change an agent’s role via the dropdown in the agents table
cases.see: The role updates and persists after reload

cases.action: Settings → Tags → create a tag with a name and colour
cases.see: The tag appears in the list with its colour dot

cases.action: Edit a tag’s name/colour
cases.see: The change is reflected in the list

cases.action: Delete a tag that is assigned to tickets
cases.see: A confirmation warns it will be removed from N tickets; confirming removes it everywhere

cases.action: Try to save a tag with an empty name
cases.see: Save is disabled/blocked

cases.action: Settings → Canned Responses → create a template with a name and rich-text body
cases.see: It appears in the list shown as a “/name” slash command

cases.action: Edit a template, then delete one (confirming)
cases.see: The edit saves; the deleted template is removed

cases.action: Try to save a template with an empty name or empty body
cases.see: Save is disabled

cases.action: Settings → SSO → toggle SSO handoff on/off
cases.see: The state saves and persists after reload

cases.action: Set the shared secret, then reveal/replace/clear it
cases.see: The secret stores masked; reveal shows it briefly; replace/clear work

cases.action: Copy the integration code snippet from the guide
cases.see: The snippet copies to clipboard with a “Copied” confirmation

cases.action: On the customer portal, sign up with a new email + password
cases.see: You’re signed in and land on the portal

cases.action: Sign out, then sign back in
cases.see: You’re signed in; your tickets show

cases.action: Sign in with the wrong password
cases.see: An error message; you stay signed out

cases.action: Sign in with Google (if configured)
cases.see: You’re signed in (otherwise skip / mark N/A)

cases.action: Without an account, start a ticket as a guest
cases.see: You can submit without signing up

cases.action: On sign-up, enter a password that misses a rule (too short / no number / no symbol) or a non-matching confirm password
cases.see: The live requirement checklist flags it and you cannot submit

cases.action: Sign out from the portal nav
cases.see: You return to the sign-in page; protected pages are no longer accessible

cases.action: As a freshly signed-up (unverified) user, find the “verify your email” banner and click Resend
cases.see: The banner shows; Resend confirms a verification email was sent

cases.action: With SSO enabled, arrive at the portal via a host-app SSO handoff link
cases.see: You land already signed in (no password prompt); an optional redirect target is honoured

cases.action: Fill in title, category, description and submit
cases.see: Confirmation screen; the ticket appears in ‘My tickets’; a confirmation email arrives

cases.action: Attach one or more files and submit
cases.see: The files show on the ticket

cases.action: Submit with the title or description empty
cases.see: Inline validation; nothing is submitted

cases.action: Attach a very large file (over the limit)
cases.see: It’s rejected with a clear message

cases.action: Fill the configured dropdown field(s) on the submit form (with search) and submit
cases.see: Selections are saved and visible on the created ticket

cases.action: Instead of a file, paste a link (e.g. Loom/Sheets) in the attachment link field and submit
cases.see: The link is attached to the ticket

cases.action: As a guest, submit with an empty or malformed email
cases.see: “Enter a valid email” error; nothing is submitted

cases.action: On the confirmation screen, copy the ticket ID and click “View your ticket”
cases.see: The ID copies (“Copied!”) and the ticket opens

cases.action: Click “Submit another ticket” on the confirmation screen
cases.see: The form resets ready for a new submission

cases.action: Open ‘My tickets’
cases.see: Your tickets are listed with their status

cases.action: Search your tickets
cases.see: The list filters to matches

cases.action: Open a ticket
cases.see: The full conversation is shown

cases.action: Post a reply
cases.see: Your reply appears in the thread

cases.action: After an agent replies, refresh / return to the ticket
cases.see: The agent’s reply appears (and/or arrives by email)

cases.action: Use the status filter tabs (All/Open/In Progress/Waiting/Resolved) on My Tickets
cases.see: The list and the per-tab counts update; an empty filter shows a clear message

cases.action: Open a resolved ticket and click “Reopen ticket”
cases.see: The composer returns and the status changes back to Open

cases.action: On a ticket, use “Copy link” and the ticket-ID copy button
cases.see: Both copy to clipboard with a “Copied!” confirmation

cases.action: Attach a file to a portal reply and send
cases.see: The attachment appears on your message in the thread

cases.action: Use “Forgot password?”, enter your email, and submit
cases.see: A neutral “if an account exists, we sent a link” confirmation (no account leak)

cases.action: Open the reset link from the email and set a new password meeting the rules
cases.see: A success screen; you can sign in with the new password

cases.action: Open the reset page without/with an invalid or expired token
cases.see: An “invalid link” message with a way to request a new one

cases.action: Open the email verification link
cases.see: An “Email verified” success screen; the verify banner disappears

cases.action: Open a stale/invalid verification link
cases.see: A clear “link expired or invalid” message with a way to request a new one

cases.action: Watch the Inbox after a customer submits a ticket
cases.see: The new ticket/conversation appears

cases.action: Open a ticket
cases.see: Conversation, customer profile, and AI analysis (sentiment/topic) are shown

cases.action: Filter / search the ticket list
cases.see: The list narrows correctly

cases.action: In the Inbox, expand/collapse a domain group and use “Show N more” / “Show fewer”
cases.see: Conversations for that domain expand/collapse; the previewed count behaves correctly

cases.action: Scroll to the bottom of a long inbox
cases.see: More conversations load; the “showing X of Y” count updates and stops at the end

cases.action: Search the inbox, then clear the search
cases.see: Results filter to matches and reset when cleared

cases.action: Combine status + category + tag filters, then click Clear
cases.see: Filters apply together; Clear resets them all

cases.action: Observe a conversation with an unread reply, then open it
cases.see: The unread dot/highlight shows, then clears after you read it

cases.action: Click a domain header/name in the inbox
cases.see: You navigate to that domain’s grouped ticket view

cases.action: Open the Inbox before any support mailbox is connected
cases.see: A “connect email” gate shows instead of an empty/broken inbox

cases.action: Send a public reply to the customer
cases.see: It appears in the thread and the customer receives it

cases.action: Add an internal note
cases.see: It’s clearly marked internal and is NOT shown to the customer / not emailed

cases.action: Try to send an empty message
cases.see: It’s blocked

cases.action: Paste a message containing script-like HTML and send
cases.see: It renders as plain text — nothing pops up or runs

cases.action: In the reply composer, type “/” and pick a canned response from the picker
cases.see: The template’s text is inserted at the cursor (the “/query” is replaced)

cases.action: Add one or more CC addresses (Enter/comma to chip them) and send a reply
cases.see: CC recipients are included on the outgoing email

cases.action: Attach a file to an agent reply and send
cases.see: The attachment shows on the sent message and reaches the customer

cases.action: Use bold/italic/list/link/code formatting and send
cases.see: Formatting is preserved in the thread (and the email)

cases.action: Type a malformed address into the CC field
cases.see: It is rejected (not added as a chip)

cases.action: Move a ticket through Open → In progress → Waiting → Resolved
cases.see: The status badge updates each time

cases.action: Change the priority
cases.see: It updates (and reorders the list if relevant)

cases.action: Assign / unassign an agent
cases.see: The assignee updates

cases.action: Add and remove tags
cases.see: Tags update

cases.action: Resolve a ticket
cases.see: It’s marked resolved; the customer receives a rating (CSAT) email

cases.action: Reopen a resolved ticket (or have the customer reply)
cases.see: It returns to an open state

cases.action: Delete a ticket
cases.see: It disappears from the lists

cases.action: Convert a NEW item into a ticket, and discard another
cases.see: They move between Inbox and Tickets / get dismissed

cases.action: On a NEW conversation, click “Convert to ticket”
cases.see: It becomes a real ticket (gets a ticket ID); a confirmation email goes to the customer

cases.action: Dismiss a NEW conversation without converting
cases.see: It leaves the default inbox; if the customer replies again it resurfaces as NEW

cases.action: Open the customer profile panel from a ticket
cases.see: Customer identity, stats, recent conversations and TMR product info show

cases.action: On a ticket whose linked GitHub issue was marked fix-deployed, reply then “Mark pending confirmation”
cases.see: The amber fix-deployed banner appears and clears after marking pending

cases.action: Open the rating link from the CSAT email
cases.see: A rating form loads

cases.action: Submit a rating and comment
cases.see: A thank-you screen; the rating shows on the ticket

cases.action: Open an invalid or expired rating link
cases.see: A graceful error, no crash

cases.action: With the dashboard open, trigger an event (new ticket / reply)
cases.see: The bell count goes up live, without refreshing

cases.action: Mark one notification read, then ‘mark all read’
cases.see: The count decrements, then goes to zero

cases.action: Have a customer send a clearly unhappy/“switching to a competitor” message, then check the bell
cases.see: A churn-risk notification appears; the ticket priority is raised with a system note

cases.action: Add the fix-deployed label on a linked GitHub issue, then check notifications
cases.see: A GitHub “needs action” notification appears for agents

cases.action: Open Customers and page through the list
cases.see: Customers with ticket counts; page 2 loads

cases.action: Open a customer
cases.see: Their tickets, notes, category, and signals are shown

cases.action: Change a customer’s category
cases.see: It updates and filtering by category works

cases.action: Add, edit, and delete a customer note
cases.see: The note changes are reflected

cases.action: Search customers and use the category filter
cases.see: The list filters to matches by name/email and category

cases.action: Open a known TMR customer’s profile and view the product section
cases.see: TMR plan/accounts/teams show (or a clear “not synced/none” state)

cases.action: Add, edit, then delete a customer note
cases.see: The note saves, updates, and is removed

cases.action: From a ticket, create a GitHub issue
cases.see: The issue is created on GitHub and linked on the ticket

cases.action: Link an existing issue number, then unlink it
cases.see: The link appears, then is removed

cases.action: Change the issue’s state on GitHub
cases.see: The linked ticket reflects the change

cases.action: Try to create a GitHub issue from a ticket before a default repo is set
cases.see: A prompt/warning to set a default repo (with a settings link); create is blocked

cases.action: In “Link existing”, enter a malformed reference (not owner/repo#123 or a GitHub URL)
cases.see: A clear inline error; nothing is linked

cases.action: Open a customer domain’s ticket view (from the inbox domain header)
cases.see: All that domain’s tickets list with counts; clicking one opens it

cases.action: Apply the status filter in the domain view, then clear it
cases.see: The list filters and resets; an empty filter shows a clear message

cases.action: Open a ticket from a known TMR customer and view the TMR product section
cases.see: Plan, accounts (with statuses) and team usage counts show

cases.action: Open a ticket whose TMR data hasn’t synced yet
cases.see: A loading/shimmer or “not synced yet” state with a way to sync

cases.action: Click the refresh/sync control on the TMR section
cases.see: It re-syncs and updates the panel

cases.action: Open a ticket from a non-TMR (external) customer
cases.see: No TMR data is shown (clean “no account linked”/hidden state, not an error)

cases.action: Open the GitHub “Action needed” page and select a notification
cases.see: The related ticket and linked issue load in the detail panel

cases.action: Send a quick reply, and use Resolve, from the action-needed panel
cases.see: The reply posts (ticket stays open) / the ticket resolves

cases.action: Use “Mark all read”
cases.see: The unread count clears

cases.action: Open the page with nothing pending
cases.see: An “all clear” empty state

cases.action: Send an email to the support address
cases.see: A ticket/conversation appears in the dashboard

cases.action: Reply to that email thread from your mail client
cases.see: It’s added to the SAME ticket, not a new one

cases.action: Have an agent reply from the dashboard
cases.see: It arrives in the customer’s mailbox, in the same thread

cases.action: Run a full archive pull, then cancel one mid-way
cases.see: Past emails import with a progress bar; cancel stops it

cases.action: Have a no-reply / bulk sender email in
cases.see: It isn’t turned into a normal support ticket

cases.action: Reply by email to an existing thread that is already a ticket
cases.see: The reply appends to the same ticket (correct threading), not a new ticket

cases.action: Send an inbound email with attachments (image/PDF)
cases.see: The files appear on the ticket and download correctly

cases.action: Create one ticket via the portal and one via email; reply to each from the dashboard
cases.see: Both customers receive correctly-threaded reply emails

cases.action: Reply to a ticket whose customer address is invalid so the mail bounces
cases.see: The ticket records a delivery-failure note; the customer is flagged as bouncing

cases.action: Submit a ticket whose answer is in your knowledge base
cases.see: Athena auto-replies with a relevant, cited answer

cases.action: Submit a question the knowledge base can’t answer
cases.see: Athena does NOT invent an answer — it escalates to an agent

cases.action: Ask something off-topic or adversarial
cases.see: It declines or escalates rather than guessing

cases.action: With shifts configured, trigger escalations
cases.see: They route to the on-shift agent; multiple escalations rotate

cases.action: Trigger an escalation when no shift is active
cases.see: It falls back sensibly — no crash

cases.action: Read a bot answer end to end
cases.see: It’s concise with a single “Learn more” link to a real KB page (no invented links)

cases.action: After the bot answers, have the customer reply that it didn’t help
cases.see: The ticket escalates to a human (assigned, status Open) with a courtesy email

cases.action: Check the customer inbox after the bot answers an emailed ticket
cases.see: The bot reply arrives threaded into the same email conversation

cases.action: Open Analytics → Operations
cases.see: Charts render with numbers that match what you’ve done

cases.action: Open Analytics → Customer insights
cases.see: Health scores, at-risk, and churn signals render

cases.action: Open Settings → AI Usage
cases.see: Token / cost totals are shown

cases.action: On the Customers dashboard, open the churn-risk and advocacy signal drawers and click a quote
cases.see: The drawer lists quotes with reasons; clicking opens the related ticket

cases.action: Hover the operations & customer charts (created-vs-resolved, sentiment, topics, agent performance)
cases.see: Tooltips show values; empty datasets show a friendly “no data yet” state

cases.action: As a non-admin agent, open Settings → AI Usage & Cost
cases.see: “Admin access required” — the data is not shown

branding.auth-branded.notes: Out of scope for initial release

branding.auth-branded-invalid.notes: Out of scope for initial release

email-setup.microsoft.notes: Out of scope for initial release

knowledge-base.check-updates.notes: I don't see any Check for updates CTA.

shifts-team.create-shift.notes: The Shift timezone dropdown not working.

portal-auth.verify-banner.notes: Please verify your email address banner still showing after verification
